purpose: Select the acquisition policy supported by the current aero-domain evidence instead of forcing one policy everywhere.

rationale: best C_D RMSE on the AirfRANS geometry-disjoint split
best top-k high-efficiency recovery on the AirfRANS geometry-disjoint split
best Pareto recall on the AirfRANS geometry-disjoint split
best Spearman efficiency rank correlation on the AirfRANS geometry-disjoint split
required_caveats: diversity still leads absolute best-design regret

rationale: best C_L RMSE on the DrivAerML geometry-heldout bridge
best top-k overlap on the DrivAerML geometry-heldout bridge
tied best Pareto recall on the DrivAerML geometry-heldout bridge
best Spearman efficiency rank correlation on the DrivAerML geometry-heldout bridge
best best-design regret on the DrivAerML geometry-heldout bridge

v2_status.interpretation: engineering_decision_utility_v2_regret_aware improves regret versus random, but does not beat diversity on this compact 3D scalar bridge.

public_claim: Mission Control chooses the acquisition strategy supported by current domain evidence.
release_scope: AirfRANS and DrivAerML use different recommended policies
DrivAerML is used as a compact 3D scalar bridge
field prediction and live solver scheduling are extension paths
